questions.question: The first recombinant DNA was created by:

options.B: Stanley Cohen and Herbert Boyer (1972)

questions.question: Restriction endonucleases cut DNA at:

options.B: Specific palindromic recognition sequences

questions.question: The first genetically engineered human insulin (Humulin) was produced using:

options.B: E. coli bacteria

questions.question: VNTR (Variable Number of Tandem Repeats) is used in:

options.B: DNA fingerprinting (DNA profiling)

questions.question: Dolly the sheep was the first mammal cloned using:

options.B: Somatic cell nuclear transfer (SCNT) from adult mammary gland cell

questions.question: Transgenic Bt cotton is resistant to insects because it produces:

options.B: Cry proteins (Bt toxin from Bacillus thuringiensis) toxic to insect larvae

questions.question: The diagram shows gel electrophoresis. How do DNA fragments separate?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of agarose gel electrophoresis: Negative electrode (cathode) at top, positive electrode (anode) at bottom. DNA loaded in wells at top (near negative). DNA is negatively charged (phosphate groups) → migrates toward positive electrode. Smaller fragments move faster through agarose pores, travel further down gel. Larger fragments move slower, remain near top. Bands visualized by ethidium bromide staining under UV light. DNA ladder (size markers) in one lane

options.B: Smaller fragments move further toward positive electrode

questions.question: Gene therapy using retrovirus as vector is used to treat:

options.B: SCID (Severe Combined Immunodeficiency) – ADA deficiency

questions.question: Golden Rice is a transgenic crop engineered to produce:

options.B: Beta-carotene (provitamin A) in endosperm

questions.question: PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) was invented by:

options.B: Kary Mullis (1983, Nobel Prize 1993)

questions.question: The diagram shows the steps of PCR. What is the purpose of the denaturation step?
questions.diagram_description: PCR cycle diagram showing three steps: (1) Denaturation at 94–95°C – hydrogen bonds between DNA strands break, two single strands formed; (2) Annealing at 50–65°C – primers bind to complementary sequences on each strand; (3) Extension at 72°C – Taq polymerase synthesizes new strand from primer using dNTPs. Each cycle doubles DNA amount. After 30 cycles: 2^30 = ~1 billion copies. Thermocycler machine shown

options.B: Separate double-stranded DNA into single strands

questions.question: Plasmids used as vectors in recombinant DNA technology must have:

options.B: Origin of replication, selectable marker (antibiotic resistance), and restriction sites

questions.question: Transgenic animals are those that have:

options.B: Foreign DNA (transgene) integrated into their genome

questions.question: In DNA fingerprinting, identical twins will have:

options.B: Identical DNA fingerprint profiles

questions.question: Sticky ends (cohesive ends) are produced by:

options.B: Restriction enzymes that make staggered cuts in palindromic DNA

questions.question: The diagram shows construction of recombinant DNA. Which enzyme joins the insert and vector?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of recombinant DNA construction: Plasmid cut with restriction enzyme (EcoRI) → linear plasmid with sticky ends. Gene of interest from donor DNA cut with same EcoRI → gene with complementary sticky ends. Sticky end annealing brings gene into plasmid. DNA ligase joins the phosphodiester bonds sealing the nicks. Recombinant plasmid formed. Transformed into E. coli. Colony selection on antibiotic plates

options.B: DNA ligase

questions.question: Cry proteins from Bacillus thuringiensis are toxic to insects because:

options.B: They form pores in insect midgut membrane causing cell lysis

questions.question: The Southern blotting technique is used to detect:

options.B: Specific DNA sequences

questions.question: CRISPR-Cas9 is a gene editing tool that:

options.B: Precisely cuts DNA at specific sequences, allowing gene editing

questions.question: The human insulin gene is expressed in bacteria using:

options.B: cDNA (complementary DNA) synthesized from mRNA, lacking introns

questions.question: The diagram shows DNA fingerprinting comparing crime scene sample with suspects. How is the DNA profile used as evidence?
questions.diagram_description: Gel electrophoresis diagram of DNA fingerprinting: Lanes labeled Crime Scene, Suspect 1, Suspect 2, Suspect 3, Victim. VNTR bands visualized. Crime scene profile matches Suspect 2 exactly (same band pattern). Suspect 1 and 3 show different patterns. The probability of two unrelated individuals having the same VNTR profile is extremely low (~1 in billions). STR (Short Tandem Repeat) analysis shown as modern method

options.B: Matching VNTR/STR band pattern between crime scene sample and suspect identifies individual

questions.question: Ti plasmid is derived from:

options.B: Agrobacterium tumefaciens (used to transform plant cells)

questions.question: Northern blotting detects:

options.B: RNA (mRNA transcripts)

questions.question: Bioreactors are used for:

options.B: Large-scale production of biological products (proteins, antibiotics, vaccines)

questions.question: Pharm animals are transgenic animals that:

options.B: Produce useful human proteins in their milk (e.g., antithrombin in goats)

questions.question: The diagram shows ELISA steps. In sandwich ELISA, what does the colour change indicate?
questions.diagram_description: Sandwich ELISA diagram: (1) Capture antibody coated on well; (2) Sample added – antigen binds if present; (3) Wash; (4) Detection antibody (enzyme-linked) added – binds antigen; (5) Wash; (6) Substrate added → enzyme converts substrate to coloured product; (7) Colour measured by spectrophotometer (absorbance). Positive well: coloured. Negative well: colourless. Quantification possible by standard curve

options.B: Presence of target antigen (positive test)

questions.question: Western blotting detects:

options.B: Specific proteins using antibodies

questions.question: The enzyme reverse transcriptase synthesizes:

options.B: DNA from RNA (cDNA synthesis)

questions.question: Knockout mice are mice in which:

options.B: A specific gene is inactivated (knocked out) to study its function

questions.question: Gene gun (biolistics) is used to:

options.B: Transform plant cells by shooting DNA-coated gold particles into cells

questions.question: The diagram shows the steps of DNA fingerprinting. Which step uses a radioactive or fluorescent probe?
questions.diagram_description: DNA fingerprinting steps: (1) DNA extraction from sample; (2) Restriction enzyme digestion of DNA at VNTR sites; (3) Gel electrophoresis – fragments separated by size; (4) Southern blot – DNA transferred from gel to nitrocellulose/nylon membrane; (5) Hybridization – radioactive/fluorescent VNTR probes added, bind complementary sequences; (6) Autoradiography – X-ray film/imaging shows bands where probe bound; (7) Band pattern compared between samples

options.B: Step 3 – Gel electrophoresis

questions.question: Recombinant hepatitis B vaccine is produced in:

options.B: Yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae – HBsAg expressed)

questions.question: A vector used in gene therapy for hereditary diseases must:

options.B: Deliver gene efficiently and safely, preferably integrate into genome without causing harm

questions.question: Bt crops have generated controversy because of concerns about:

options.B: Effects on non-target organisms, biodiversity, and gene flow to wild plants

questions.question: The Taq polymerase used in PCR is obtained from:

options.B: Thermus aquaticus (thermophilic bacterium from hot springs)

questions.question: Erythropoietin (EPO) produced by recombinant DNA technology is used to treat:

options.B: Anaemia in kidney failure patients

questions.question: The probe in Southern hybridization is:

options.B: Single-stranded labeled DNA/RNA complementary to target sequence

questions.question: Insulin produced by recombinant DNA technology differs from porcine insulin in that it:

options.B: Is identical to human insulin (no allergic reaction risk)

questions.question: The 'selectable marker' in a cloning vector allows:

options.B: Selection of transformed bacteria (antibiotic resistance gene kills non-transformed cells)

questions.question: STR (Short Tandem Repeat) analysis is more commonly used than VNTR in forensics because:

options.B: STRs can be analyzed from very small/degraded samples using PCR

questions.question: The first human gene therapy trial (1990) treated:

options.B: ADA-SCID (adenosine deaminase deficiency)

questions.question: Microarray (DNA chip) technology is used for:

options.B: Simultaneous expression analysis of thousands of genes

questions.question: The plasmid pBR322 carries resistance genes for:

options.B: Ampicillin and tetracycline

questions.question: RNA interference (RNAi) works by:

options.B: Silencing specific genes using dsRNA that degrades complementary mRNA

questions.question: The first transgenic animal produced was:

options.B: A mouse (1974, Rudolf Jaenisch)

questions.question: The diagram shows insulin gene cloning. Why is cDNA used instead of genomic DNA for expressing insulin in bacteria?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram comparing: (A) Genomic insulin gene – contains introns (non-coding, processed out in eukaryotes) which bacteria CANNOT remove → non-functional protein. (B) cDNA approach: isolate insulin mRNA from pancreatic beta cells → reverse transcriptase makes cDNA → cDNA has no introns → insert into expression vector → bacteria can transcribe and translate correctly → functional human insulin produced

options.B: Bacteria cannot process introns, so intron-free cDNA is needed for functional expression
options.C: cDNA is easier to cut with restriction enzymes

questions.question: Gene gun uses which particles to carry DNA into plant cells?

options.B: Gold or tungsten microparticles coated with DNA

questions.question: The 'Rosie' transgenic cow produced:

options.B: Human alpha-lactalbumin in milk (nutritionally enriched for human babies)

questions.question: Bioinformatics involves:

options.B: Computational analysis and management of biological data (genomics, proteomics)

questions.question: The Human Genome Project was completed in:
